Technical Assistance •• Admlnistratian •• (916) 322·5662 322-5660 June 29, 1984 W. J. Anthony, Director Department of General Services State & Consumer Services Agency 915 Capitol Mall, Suite 590 Sacramento, CA 95814 Executive/legal •• Enfarcement 322.5901 322-6441 Re: Your Request for Advice Our No. A-84-l59 Dear Mr. Anthony: You have written requesting our advice on whether discounted air fares for personal travel by state officials is reportable income for those officials if the discount opportunity is negotiated by the state as part of its contract with commercial carriers for reduced-fare air travel for state employees and officials traveling on state business. We understand that you have submitted a separate request to the Attorney General on the issue of whether or not this is permissible under California Constitution, Art. 12, Section 19. Because the Attorney General's Opinion could determine that such a program is prohibited by the Constitution, your question may be moot. Therefore, we decline to respond to it until the Attorney General has acted. If the Attorney General determines that such a program is permissible, we will then act promptly to give you advice. Sincerely, ~~:.2d7'1- Counsel Legal Division REL:plh Statf> of California State and Consumer Services Agency Memorandum Date To May 29, 1984 Dan Stanford, Chairperson Fair Political Practices Commission 1100 K Street -- 0-23 File No.: Subject: From Department of General Services On May 3, 1984, I asked for your advice on whether an officer and employee of the State, taking advantage of the discounted rates for personal trips, under the contracts being consummated with PSA and Delta Airlines, will have to report the dollar value of the discounted rate under your rules. We have now determined that the extension of the discounted rates to management personnel for personal trips will not be included in the con- tract for this year. However, we would appreciate your advice on the question asked in our memorandum, inasmuch as we will have that problem before us as we enter into contract negotiations next year. If you wish more information on this subject, you may wish to have your staff contact: Jack Smith, Chief, Office of Program and Compliance Evalua- tion (323-3066), or Emil J. Relat, Chief Counsel, Office of Legal Services (322-5944) . W. J. L Under the , contract, the airlines will extend the dfscounted rates to management per- sonnel of the State when they travel on personal matt~rs .. We would 'appreciate your early advice on ,whether an officer or an employee of the State, who takes advantage of the discounted'rate, will have to report the dollar value of the discounted rate under your rules. If you wish more infonnation on this subject, you may wish to have your staff contact: Jack Smith, Chief, Office of Program and Compliance Evalua- tion (323-3066), or Emil J. ANllfONY, Director Department of General Services WJA:EJR:dh cc: Jack Smith, Chief Office of Program and Compliance Evaluation I STArE OF CALIFORNIA-STATE AND CONSUMER SERVICES AGENCY DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES EXECUTIVE OFFICE 915 CAPITOl MAll, SUITE 590 SACRAMENTO, CA 95814 February 15, 1984 TO PROSPECTIVE BIDDERS: GEORGE DEUKMEJIAN, Governor Attached is the addenda for Request for Proposal DGS-84-001 entitled Discounted Air Fares for State Travel. The addenda has been developed from information received at the February 10, 1984 Bidders Conference held in Sacramento. If you have any questions, please contact Susan Hogg in Sacramento at (916 )?23-1~. Il~l ') / n:~~~~ ~. ~{/1 . I~ •• Smi th, Chi f I, ompliance Evaluation \ .. JDS = SH:al f Attachment STATE OF CALIFORNIA-STATE AND CONSUMER SERVICES AGENCY ~DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES EXECUTIVE OFFICE 915 CAPITOL MAll, SUITE 590 SACRAMENTO, CA 95814 January 31, 1984 TO PROSPECTIVE BIDDERS: GEORGE DEUKMEJIAN, GoWlrnor HAY 15 8 AM 04 You are invited to review and respond to the enclosed Request for Proposal DGS-84-001 entitled Discounted Air Fares for State Travel. If you have any questions, please contact Susan Hogg in Sacramento at (916)323-3071. Attachment REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL FOR DISCOUNTED AIR FARES FOR STATE TRAVEL RFP DGS-84-001 STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES OFFICE OF PROGRAM AND COMPLIANCE EVALUATION 818 K STREET MALL, SUITE 100 SACRAMENTO, CA 95814 TABLE OF CONTENTS INTRODUCTION AND OVERVIEW OF REQUIREMENTS RULES GOVERNING COMPETITION SERVICE TO BE PERFORMED COST PROPOSAL FORMAT EVALUATION ONE PERCENT SAMPLE OF 1979-80 TRAVEL CLAIMS FOR AIR TRANSPORTATION FOR PRIMARY CITY-PAIRS ONE PERCENT SAMPLE OF 1979-80 TRAVEL CLAIMS FOR AIR TRANSPORTATION FOR REMAINDER OF CITY-PAIRS SCHEDULE OF ITEMS INSTRUCTIONS FOR SCREENING FINAL PROPOSALS STANDARD FORM 2 PAGE 2 SECTION I SECTION II SECTION III SECTION IV SECTION V SECTION VI ATTACHMENTS A B C D E PAGE 3 SECTION I INTRODUCTION AND OVERVIEW OF REQUIREMENTS A. Purpose Of This Request for Proposal The Department of General Services has been mandated by the Budget Act of 1983-84, Chapter 324, Item 1760-001-666 to '. .negotiate air fare discounts. .' for state travel. These discounted fares would be used for authorized state business between the city-pairs shown in Attachment A. A city-pair for purposes of this RFP is defined as travel between two destinations. For example, the city-pair of Sacramento to los Angeles International requires flights from Sacramento to los Angeles International and from los Angeles International to Sacramento. The State currently spends $20 million annually for airline travel. Based on a Department of General Services study conducted on travel patterns for the 1979-80 fiscal year, there are approximately 222,300 one-way flights made each year for state business. Of this total, 176,800 flights costing approximately $11 million are necessary for employee travel primarily between Northern and Southern California. The specific airports affected are as follows: Sacramento; San Francisco; Oakland; San Jose; los Angeles International; Burbank; Orange County; Ontario; and San Diego. The purpose of this Request for ProposalCRFP) is to obtain discounted air fares from air carriers. The specific city-pairs identified in Attachment A represent the primary travel needs of the State. The additional routes identified in Attachment e represent the remainder of State travel needs based on the sample of the 1979-80 travel patterns. B. Official Department Contact The Department Official contact person to whom to send proposals or copies of protests to, or ask questions to, is: Susan Hogg Department of General Services Office of Program and Compliance Evaluation 818 K Street Mall, Suite 100 Sacramento, CA 95814 Telephone: (916) 323-3071 PAGE 4 C. Key Action Dates listed below are the important actions and dates, and times in which actions must be taken or completed. If the State finds it necessary to change any of these dates, it will be accomplished by addendum. Task 1. Release of RFP 2. Bidders' Conference 3. Confidential discussion with individual contractors, if requested 4. last day to protest RFP 5. Submission of Final Proposal 6. Opening of proposals 7. Public notification of 8. 9. 10. intent to award last day to protest selection Contract awardCs) and execution ContractorCs) begin work Date 1-31-84 2-10-84 1-31-84 to 2-17-84 2-17-84 3-16-84 3-16-84 4-5-84 4-12-84 4-13-84 7-1-84 Ti me 10:00 a.m. 2:00 p.m. 2:00 p.m. PAGE 5 SECTION II RULES GOVERNING COMPETITION A. Proposing Requirements and Conditions 1. General This Request for Proposal, the evaluation of responses, and the award of any resultant contractCs) shall be made in conformance with the rules of this section and applicable State law and policy. A bidders Final Proposal will be taken as an irrevocable offer for 30 days following the scheduled date for contract award specified in Section I. 2. Request for Proposa 1 Documents This RFP includes, in addition to an explanation of the State's needs which must be met, instructions which prescribe the format and content of the proposal to be submitted. 3. Examination of the Work The bidders should carefully examine the entire RFP and addenda thereto, and all related materials and data referenced in the RFP or otherwise available, and should become fully aware of the nature and location(s) of the services, the quantities of the services, and the conditions to be encountered in providing the services. 4. Bidders' Conference The Bidders' Conference will be held at the Department of General Services, 915 Capitol Mall, Room 587, Sacramento, CA, 95814, on February 10, 1984, at 10:00 a.m. The purpose of the Bidders' Conference is to provide potential contractors an opportunity to obtain clarification of the intent or content of this RFP. 5. Questions Regarding the RFP Bidders requiring clarification of the intent or content of this RFP may contact the Department Official listed in Section I, paragraph B. 6. Confidential Discussions with Individual Bidders PAGE 6 A potential contractor may request confidential discussions in accordance with the dates specified in Section 1. The purpose of these discussions is to afford bidders the opportunity to meet with state personnel to the discuss the RFP and the procurement process. B. Steps of Proposal Development and Review 1. Final Proposal The Final Proposal must information and signatures agreement. 2. Submission of Proposals be complete, on the cover including letters and all cost standard The instructions contained herein apply to the Final Proposal. a. Preparation Proposals are to be prepared in such a way as to provide a straightforward, concise delineation of capabilities to satisfy the requirements of this RFP. EMPHASIS SHOULD BE CONCENTRATED ON CONFORMANCE TO THE RFP INSTRUCTIONS, RESPONSIVENESS TO THE RFP REQUIREMENTS, AND ON COMPLETENESS AND CLARITY OF CONTENT. b. Bidders' Costs Cost for developing proposals are the responsibility entirely of the bidder and shall not be chargeable to the State. c. Completion of Proposa1a Proposals must be complete in all respects as required by the RFP section on PROPOSAL FORMAT. A Final Proposal may be rejected if it is conditional or incomplete, or if there are any alterations of form or other irregularities of any kind. d. Signature of Proposal A cover letter, which shall be considered an integral part of the Final Proposal, shall be signed by an individualCs) who is(are) authorized to bind the bidder(s) contractually. The signatureCs) must indicate the title or position that the individualCs) holds in the firm. The bidder(s) must not include any limiting statements in the cover letter that would preclude the Final Proposal from being held as an irrevocable offer for at least 30 days after the scheduled award date. An unsigned Final Proposal shall be rejected. A Final Proposal may be signed by an agentCs) of the bidder(s).Q..n.lx. if the agent is an officer or a corporate contractor authorized to sign PAGE 7 contracts on bidders behalf, a member of a partnership, or is properly authorized by a power of attorney or equivalent document. Such authorization must be submitted to the State prior to the submission of proposals or with the proposals. The name and title of the individualsCs) signing the proposal must be typed immediately below the signature. e. Delivery of Proposals Mail or deliver proposals to the the Department of General Services, Office of Program and Compliance Evaluation, 818 K Street Mall, Suite 100, Sacramento, CA, 95814, Attention: Susan Hogg. If mailed, use certified or registered mail with return receipt requested. Proposals received after 2:00 p.m., March 16, 1984, will not be considered. Proposals must be received in the number of copies stated in the RFP section on PROPOSAL FORMAT, and not later than the dates and times specified in SECTION I, paragraph C. One copy must be clearly marked Proposal for Discounted Air Fares and 'Master COpy'. They shall also be clearly marked 'CONFIDENTIAL' and shall state the scheduled date and time for submission. Final Proposals not received by the date and time specified in Section I, or not sealed, will be rejected. Proposals submitted under improperly marked covers may be rejected. If discrepancies are found between two or more copies of the proposal, the proposal may be rejected. However, if not so rejected, the 'Master Copy' will provide the basis for resolving such discrepancies. BIDDERS ARE TO SUBMIT THEIR PROPOSALS WITH THE BID PRICE AND ALL COST INFORMATION IN A SEPARATE, SEALED ENVELOPE. f. Withdrawal and Resubmission/Modification of Proposals A bidder may withdraw the proposal at any time prior to final bid due date by submitting a written not"ification of its withdrawal signed by the bidder or agent authorized in accordance with paragraph B 2 d of this section (Signature of Proposal). He/she may thereafter submit a new or modified proposal prior to final bid due date. Modification offered in any other manner, oral or written, will not be considered. A Final Proposal cannot be changed or withdrawn after the time designated for receipt, except as provided in paragraph 'f c of this section. g. Joint Proposal A joint proposal, that is, two or more bidders quoting jointly on one proposal, may be submitted. Each participating bidder must sign the joint proposal. If the contract is awarded to joint contractors, it shall be one indivisible contract. Each joint contract will be jointly and severally responsible for the performance of the entire contract, and the joint contractors must designate, in writing, one individual having authority to PAGE 8 represent them in all matters relating to the contract. The state assumes no responsibility or obligation for the division of orders or purchases among the joint contractors. 3. Rejection of Proposals The State may reject any or all proposals, and mayor may not waive any immaterial deviation or defect in a proposal. The State's waiver of an immaterial deviation or defect shall in no way modify the RFP documents or excuse the contractor from full compliance with the RFP specifications if he/she is awarded the contract. 4. Evaluation and Selection Process j;!. General Proposals wi 11 be evaluated according to the procedures contained in the RFP, Section VI, EVALUATION. The opening of Final Proposals will be conducted at the Department of General Services, 915 Capitol Mall, Room 587, Sacramento, CA, 95814, on March 16, 1984, at 2:00 p.m. b. Evaluation Questions During the evaluation and selection process, the State may desire the presence of a bidders representative for the purpose of answering specific questions, orally and/or in writing. c. Errors in the Final Proposal If discrepancies between sections or other errors are found in a Final Proposal, the State may reject the proposal; however, the State may, at its sole option, retain the proposal and correct any arithmetic errors in price on the basis that the proposal narrative shall have priority over proposed contract clauses; the proposed contract clauses shall have priority over the cost sheets, and within each of these, the highest level of detail will prevail. If an item is described in the narrative and omitted from the proposed contract and cost data provided in the Final Proposal for evaluation purposes, it will be presumed to be provided by the contractor at no cost. IT IS ABSOLUTELY ESSENTIAL THAT THE BIDDER CAREFULLY REVIEW THE COST ELEMENTS IN THE BIDDERS' FINAL PROPOSAL, SINCE THE BIDDER WILL NOT HAVE THE OPTION TO CHANGE THEM AFTER THE TIME FOR SUBMITTAL. 5. AwardCs) of ContractCs) PAGE 9 AwardCs) of contract(s), if made, will be to the responsible bidder selected in accordance with the RFP Section on EVALUATION, and whose Final ProposalCs) complies with all the requirements of the RFP documents and addenda thereto, except for such immaterial defects as may be waived by the State. Award(s), if made, 101111 be made within thirty (30) days after the scheduled date for contract award specified in Section I, paragraph C. Written notification of the State's intent to award will be made to all bidders submitting proposals. If a contractor, having submitted a Final Proposal, can show that they, instead of the bidder selected by the State, should be selected for contract/award according to the rules of paragraph B ¥ of this section, the bidder will be allowed five (5) working days to submit a protest to the intent to award, paragraph D 1 of this section. The State reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to exclude from any contracts awarded certain city-pairs. C. Contractual Information 1. Contract Form The State of California has a standard agreement (Standard Form 2) which must be agreed upon by each bidder submitting a proposal in response to this RFP. The standard agreement will incorporate the attached terms and conditions(Attachment E), this RFP, the bidder's response to this RFP, and a nondiscrimination clause(Attachment E)' Terms and conditions which do not comply in substance with all material requirements of the RFP, which are contrary to the best interests of the State, or which are in opposition to State policy, wi 11 not be accepted and wi 11 be cause for rejection of the proposal. 2. Term of Contract The State intends to retain the required services for one year. The contract may be modified by mutual consent of both the State and the contractor. 3. Termination or Cancellation The State reserves the right to cancel any contract or contracts awarded pursuant to this RFP upon 30 days notice. p. Other Information 1. Protests PAGE 10 If a bidder has submitted a proposal which the bidder believes to be totally responsive to the requirements of the RFP and believes the State has incorrectly selected another conractor for awardCs), a protest of the selection may be submitted as described below. All protests must be made in writing, signed by an individual authorized under paragraph B 2. d of this section (Signature of Proposal), and contain a statement of the reason(s) for protest, citing the law, rule, regulation, or procedures on which the protest is based. The protestor must provide facts and evidence to support the claim. Protests must be mailed or delivered to the Department of General Services, Office of Program and Compliance Evaluation, 818 K Street, Suite 100, Sacramento, CA, 95814, Attention: Susan Hogg. All protests to the RFP or protests concerning the evaluation, recommendation, or other aspects of the selection process must be received as promptly as possible, but not later than the respectiVe times and dates specified in Section I, paragraph C, for such protests. Certified or registered mail must be used unless delivered in person, in which case the protestor should obtain a receipt of delivery. 2. News Re 1 eases News releases pertaining to the award resulting from the RFP shall not be made without prior written approval of Department of General Services, Office of Program and Compliance Evaluation, 818 K Street Mall, Suite 100, Sacramento, CA, 95814, Attention: Susan Hogg. 3. Disposition of Proposals All materials submitted in response to this RFP will become the property of the State of California and will be returned, if requested by a contractor, only at the State's option and the contractor's expense. The Master Copy shall be retained for official files and will become a public record after the date and time for Final Proposal submission as specified in Section I, paragraph C (KEY ACTION DATES), confidential financial information submitted in support of the requirement to show contractor responsibility will be returned upon request. 4. Contacts for Information Contractors may contact the Department Official listed in Section I, paragraph B, for purposes of familiarization with the RFP requirements. Visits shall be made by appointment only, during normal business hours, and will be limited to the Department Official or her designee(s). PAGE 11 ORAL COMMUNICATIONS of Department officers and employees concerning the RFP SHALL NOT BE BINDING ON THE STATE and SHALL IN NO WAY EXCUSE THE CONTRACTOR OF OBLIGATIONS as set forth in the RFP. PAGE 12 SECTION III SERVICE TO BE PROVIDED A. Introductjon This section contains the technical requirements pertaining to the proposal. See Section II, RULES GOVERNING COMPETITION, and Section V, PROPOSAL FORMAT for other requirements that must be met in order to be considered responsive to this RFP. B. Goals The goals of the discounted air fares program are: 1. To reduce the travel expend i tures of the State of Cal i forn i a. 2. To obtain discounted air fares for authorized State employees. C. Scope of Contract This contract transportation for travelers. provides selected for the city-pair furnishing flights for of air passenger aut hor i zed State The travel patterns identified in Attachments A and B are based on a study conducted by the State of California from a IX sample of 1979-80 travel claims. The estimated travelers per year are furnished to bidders for informational purposes only and shall not be construed to represent any amount which the State of California shall be obligated to purchase under the contract. In the event the State of California's future requirements for air transportation services do not equal the amounts listed as 'estimated travelers per year', or if agencies and employees fail to comply with the contract, such failure shall not constitute a breach of contract nor shall it be a basis for an equitable adjustment. D. ContractorCs)' Responsibilities 1. Mandatory Regul rements a. The contractorCs) will furnish the authorized State traveler with the same services provided to the general public in unrestricted scheduled jet coach service, subject to the rules and procedures published in the air carrier's tariffs on file with the Airline Tariff Publishing Company. The contractor shall make reservations for authorized State travelers on the same basis as for regular coach service travelers and shall not discriminate in favor of commercial travelers. If the contractor PAGE 13 offers a fare lower than the contract fare, the State of California may use the lower fare in lieu of the contract fare. The contractor shall have contract fare quote data Cbid price) entered into its electronic reservation system. Contractors mus t f urn ish f 1 i g ht schedu 1 es on awa rd ed routes to the Depa rtment Official and private travel agencies, if designated by the State of California, after award. b. The fares awarded will be firm for the duration of the contract. The fares must be individuallY bid on each city-pair identified in Attachment A. c. The contractor shall report to the Department of General Services, Office of Fleet Administration, 1416 Tenth Street, 2nd Floor, Sacramento, 95814, the total number of authorized State travelers and total revenue. The contractorCs)' report shall be provided quarterly by city-pair, with a monthly breakdown. The contractor performance report is due no later than the twentieth (20) day of the month following the end of the report period. The successful bidder(s) will be furnished with the detailed requirements of this report. d. Contractors are required to fully refund any or all portions of an unused ticket to either the State of California, or private travel agencies if so designated by the State of California. e. Bidders must include the cost of meals and baggage handling in the fare bid. f. Federal, State, and local taxes are to be included in all price bids. g. Bidders agree to accept all business under any contract awarded as a result of this RFP. 2. Desirable Qualifications a. The bidders may propose discounted air fares for authorized State travelers on intra and interstate flights as identified in Attachment B or for routes not specified in this RFP. b. The bidders may propose the provision of special services to State management employees (approximately 3,000), PAGE 14 ;So Bidders' Overall Abillty to Perform Each bidder must demonstrate its capability of fulfilling the potential travel volumes and the flight availability for the State to carry out its mission. Flight availabil1ty for purposes of this RFP and contract awardCs) shall be that flight scheduling as determined by the State to best meet the bus i ness needs of the State. 4. Contract Period The contract is for the period of July 1, 1984 through June 30, 1985. 5. Submission of Bids a. For each item bid, bidders must furnish the information specified in Attachment C. The items bid must make available to the State of California authorized travelers flights between city-pairs without any restriction. The State of California will not consider bids which are conditioned upon an eligibility requirement such as time limitation for advance purchase of tickets, day or time of departure, length of stay, capacity control or ticket cancellation charge. b. Bidders must provide bids on at least one city-pair identified in Attachment A jf the bidder provides service to that route. Bids for the additional routes identified in Attachment B or those not specified must be accompanied by a bid for at least one item in Attachment A unless the air carrier does not provide service to H..!l.Y of the city-pairs identified in Attachment A. For those carriers who do not provide service on H..!l.Y routes identified in Attachment A, bids will be accepted solelY for routes identified in Attachment B or those not specified. In those instances, a statement to that affect must accompany the Final Proposal. 6. Ordering of Services a. The State of California may enter into agreements with various commercial travel agents to provide travel management services. These contractors are responsible for arranging and providing all travel services to each participating State agency in regard to air transportation travel. The successful bidders from this RFP will be furnished a listing of the participating travel agencies. Full bid discounts for authorized State travel must be made aval lable through these travel agencies. PAGE 15 b. The detail of ordering of services will be developed in conjunction with the participating travel agencies. The detailed instructions for ordering of services will be furnished to the successful bidders of this RFP. E. Responsibility of State of California in Relatjon to Proposal 1. The State of California will disseminate to State employees the mandatory requirements and procedures for use of service along with all information relative to the schedules of the awarded contractors. 2. The State of California, for individual trips of State employees, will not be required to obtain services from the contractorCs) if: (1) flight schedules do not meet the employees' mission requirements; (2) space is not available in time to accomplish the mission requirements; or (3) flight schedules would require additional overnight lodging. PAGE 16 SECTION IV COST A. Fiscal limits The State of California is not committed to guarantee any minimum amount of bus i ness for any contracts awarded. B. Method of Payment 1. The payment for air transportation services will be made directly by a State of California agency or designated travel agencies under contract with the State of California. 2. The payments will be due on the thirtieth (30) calendar day after the date of actual receipt of a proper invoice in the office(s) designated (State Administrative Manual, Section 8422). These designations will be furnished to the successful bidders. 3. Invoices shall be submitted in an original and two copies to the State of California, or to the travel agencies so designated by the State. To constitute a proper invoice, the invoice must include the following information and/or attached documentation: name of business concern and invoice date; contract number or other authorization for delivery of services; description, price, and quantity of services actually delivered or rendered; shipping and payment terms; other SUbstantiating documentation or information as required by the contract; name where practicable, title, phone number, and complete mailing address of responsible official to whom payment is to be sent. PAGE 17 SECTION V PROPOSAL FORMAT A. Introduction These instruct ions prescr i be the mandatory proposal format and the approach for the development and presentation of proposal data. Format instructions must be adhered to; all requirements and questions in the RFP must be responded to; and all requested data must be supplied. B. Final Proposal FormClt and Content The complete bid must be submitted in the number of copies indicated (one copy stamped 'Master Copy') and include the following items: 1. Cover Letter - 4 cop; es Must be prepared and signed in accordance with Section II, paragraph B 2.. d, Signature of Proposal, and included as first page of Volume I. 2. Volume I - Response to Requirements - 4 copies This volume must contain all responses to the REQUIREMENTS OF THE RFP, INCLUDING SECTION III, SERVICE TO BE PROVIDED. All information, except for cost data, must be completed and included in this volume. This includes completed sheets as shown in Attachment C. 3. Vo I ume I I - Comp 1 eted Contract - 4 cop i es This volume must contain the completed and signed standard agreement, Standard Form 2(See Attachment E)' 4. Volume III - Cost Proposal 4 copies This volume must contain all completed cost information required by this RFP. This volume must be sealed and submitted in an envelope separate from the other volumes. This is a separate item from Attachment C that identifies.Q..!J...l.y air fares by city-pair.- A. Receipt SECTION VI EVALUATION PAGE 18 Each proposal will be date and time marked as it is received and verified that it is properly sealed. Proposals will remain sealed until the designated time for opening. B. Evaluation of Final Proposals 1. Proposal Opening and Validation Check All proposals received by the time and date specified in Section I, Paragraph C (KEY ACTION DATES), will be opened at the same time. Sub- sequently, the proposals will be checked for the presence of the required information in conformance with the requirements of this RFP. Absence of required information will deem the proposal nonresponsive and may cause rejection. Specific instructions for opening and validation of the Final Proposals are contained in Attachment 0, "Instructions for Screening Final Proposals." 2. Validation Against Requirements The State will check each proposal in detail to determine its compliance to the RFP requirements. 3. Bidders' Ability to Perform If during the evaluation process, the State is unable to assure itself of a bidders' ability to perform under the contract if awarded, the State has the option of requesting from the bidder any information which the State deems necessary to determine the bidders' responsibility. If such information is required, the bidder will be so notified and will be permitted approximately five working days to submit the information required. If the information submitted by the bidder, or available from the other sources, is insufficient to satisfy the State as to the bidders' responsibility, the State may ask for additional information or reject the proposal, The State's determination of a bidders' responsibility, for the purposes of the RFP, shall be final. 4. Fare Analysis The required fare information will be checked for completeness and accuracy. Errors and inconsistencies will be dealt with according to procedures contained in Section II, Paragraph B 4 c, Errors in the Final Proposal. PAGE 19 5. Evaluation Committee A committee of State employees will be designated to evaluate the Final Proposals received in regard to this RFP. 6. Format a. All Final Proposals will be reviewed to determine those that are eligible. "Eligible" is defined as a Final Proposal which meets the format requirements and the standards specified in the RFP. b. Those eligible proposals shall then be submitted to the Evalua- tion Committee which must evaluate and score the proposals using the method specified in Paragraph 7 of this section. c. All proposals, evaluation, and scoring sheets will be available to the public at the conclusion of the scoring process. 7. Selection a. Award of contract(s), if made, will be to the responsible bidder receiving the highest overall score on individual city-pairs computed in accordance with SECTION VI, EVALUATION, except for those instances identified in Paragraph h of this section. b. Formal selection will be on the basis of three factors: (1) lowest fares on individual city-pairs; (2) best flight availability for the purposes of carrying out the missions of the State of California; and (3) desirable qualifications identified in Section III, Paragraph 0 2. c. Each response to this RFP will be subject to the same review and assessment process by the Evaluation Committee. d. Each city-pair will be individually evaluated. e. Points within each category will be awarded on a proportionate basis. The categories are: (1) fares, (2) flight availability, and (3) desirable qualifications. That bid which has the lowest fare for a city-pair will be allotted the highest points. That bid which has the highest fare for a city-pair will be allotted the lowest points. That bid which best meets the requirements of flight avail- ability will be allotted the highest points. That bid which least meets the requirements of flight availability will be allotted the lowest poi nts. That bid which best meets the requirements of desirable qualifications will be allotted the highest points. That bid which least meets the requirements of desirable qualifications will be allocated the lowest points. PAGE 20 All other bids will be allotted points proportionately to the manner in which the bid requirements are met. Categorical points for each city-pair will be added so as to determine that bid which shall receive the highest overall score. Points will be allotted for the following categories: Fares Flight Availability (SEC. III, Para. 03) Desirable Qualifications (SEC. III, Para. D) Total Maximum 50 points Maximum 30 points Maximum 20 points 100 points f. Points will be designated for fares for those city-pairs identified in Attachment A and Attachment B and those routes not specified. g. Bidders with the highest score on individual city-pairs will be awarded a contract. A bidder could be awarded a contract for more than one city-pair. h. No more than one award will be made on each individual city- pair except for those city-pairs with estimated travel of 15,000 or more one-way trips per year as identified in Attachment A. The State will utilize the successful contractor(s) for State travel exclusively except as stated in Section III, Paragraph E 2. i. In view of the anticipated State need for airline services at various times, the capacity of any single airline to accomodate all requirements of this RFP may be insufficient. Therefore, it is anticipated that for those city-pairs with estimated travel of 15,000 or more one-way trips per year as identified in Attachment A, progressive awards may be made. For these city-pairs, two contracts will be awarded. A primary contract shall be awarded to the bidder with the highest score and be utilized unless the contractor is un- able to accomodate the State's travel needs. The bidder with the next highest score will be awarded a secondary contract and will agree to accept business at the price(s) quoted. 8. Proposal Rejection The State reserves the right at any time to reject any or all proposals. ...... ,.; . I FPPC TRANSMITTAL OF CORRESPONDENCE CORRESPONDENT (J! IUfi!l'&J V(/W /7 /) (J . sections.conclusion: Because the Attorney General's Opinion could determine that such a program is prohibited by the Constitution, your question may be moot. Therefore, we decline to respond to it until the Attorney General has acted. If the Attorney General determines that such a program is permissible, we will then act promptly to give you advice.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether discounted air fares for personal travel by state officials negotiated as part of state contracts with commercial carriers constitute reportable income under FPPC rules.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to respond to the question pending the Attorney General's constitutional determination regarding the permissibility of the discounted air fare program, noting the question may become moot depending on that opinion.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains multiple copies of the same FPPC response letter (June 29, 1984) along with supporting memoranda from Department of General Services (May 3 and May 29, 1984) and RFP documentation. The FPPC response is a declination letter, not a substantive advice letter. OCR quality is good overall with some minor artifacts.

embedding.first_500_words: Technical Assistance •• Admlnistratian •• (916) 322·5662 322-5660 June 29, 1984 W. J. Anthony, Director Department of General Services State & Consumer Services Agency 915 Capitol Mall, Suite 590 Sacramento, CA 95814 Executive/legal •• Enfarcement 322.5901 322-6441 Re: Your Request for Advice Our No. A-84-l59 Dear Mr. Anthony: You have written requesting our advice on whether discounted air fares for personal travel by state officials is reportable income for those officials if the discount opportunity is negotiated by the state as part of its contract with commercial carriers for reduced-fare air travel for state employees and officials traveling on state business. We understand that you have submitted a separate request to the Attorney General on the issue of whether or not this is permissible under California Constitution, Art. 12, Section 19. Because the Attorney General's Opinion could determine that such a program is prohibited by the Constitution, your question may be moot. Therefore, we decline to respond to it until the Attorney General has acted. If the Attorney General determines that such a program is permissible, we will then act promptly to give you advice. Sincerely, ~~:.2d7'1- Counsel Legal Division REL:plh Statf> of California State and Consumer Services Agency Memorandum Date To May 29, 1984 Dan Stanford, Chairperson Fair Political Practices Commission 1100 K Street -- 0-23 File No.: Subject: From Department of General Services On May 3, 1984, I asked for your advice on whether an officer and employee of the State, taking advantage of the discounted rates for personal trips, under the contracts being consummated with PSA and Delta Airlines, will have to report the dollar value of the discounted rate under your rules. We have now determined that the extension of the discounted rates to management personnel for personal trips will not be included in the con- tract for this year. However, we would appreciate your advice on the question asked in our memorandum, inasmuch as we will have that problem before us as we enter into contract negotiations next year. If you wish more information on this subject, you may wish to have your staff contact: Jack Smith, Chief, Office of Program and Compliance Evalua- tion (323-3066), or Emil J. Relat, Chief Counsel, Office of Legal Services (322-5944) . W. J. embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter (A-84-159) from June 29, 1984, declining to provide advice on whether discounted personal air fares for state officials constitute reportable income, pending the Attorney General's determination on whether such a program is constitutionally permissible under California Constitution Article 12, Section 19.
